title: Effectiveness of an online, dyadic, culturally adapted physical activity intervention for south Asian mothers and daughters (SAATH): a randomised controlled trial.
abstract: South Asian females in the United States experience elevated cardiometabolic risk and have lower levels of moderate-to-vigorous intensity physical activity (MVPA) than other race and ethnic groups. This study evaluated the effectiveness of a dyadic, culturally adapted behavioral intervention to increase MVPA among US South Asian mothers and adolescent daughters.
